channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish collection missed, unable to report, 4th June
item.description:
On Saturday it was very hot and the binmen did not collect this rubbish. They lied and marked it as excessive waste with no boxes so I could not report it as a missed collection. I was automatically advised to have more containers, you can see all our cardboard has been neatly packed within the boxes ready for collection. I appreciate it was very hot and working in those conditions is difficult but I have a right for the rubbish to be collected. We have no other way of disposing this and you only come twice a month despite all the council tax we pay. The cardboard is just going to get excessively worse every two weeks if you do not collect this. It’s incredibly disappointing that this was missed. I followed all the rules.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 27, Anerley Hill, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Prickly overgrown brushes catching everyone, 4th June
item.description:
There are over grown prickly brushes blocking the pathway over the bridge. They are on both sides. These are spikey and will injure someone else - specially little people who use it for school
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Longmeadow Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox in the middle of the road, 4th June
item.description:
There is a dead fox on the road leading towards rotherhithe tunnel, after the traffic lights, in the car lane.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 281, Jamaica Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Dead animal in the carriageway or footway

item.title: Broken glass and litter in street, 4th June
item.description:
Street cleaners did not pick up litter on their weekly schedule. Ongoing problem that requires monitoring, Serco not delivering the service they are contracted to do

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49, Combermere Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please can this fridge be removed please?, 4th June
item.description:
Can this dumped fridge be removed please?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ethelbert Close, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Protruding metal cover in middle of the road, 4th June
item.description:
Elmington Road just before you get to the two road signs either side (see photo of the road signs at Fix My Street Case Reference Number 9514294). Of danger to both cyclists and other vehicles. In daylight it might be seen and avoided but at night time, any cyclist, particularly approaching from the Stacey Path end of Elmington Road, will be thrown off their bicycle as the cover is protruding upwards out of the road by about 1-2 inches.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmington Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped car, 4th June
item.description:
This car has been in this spot for months. I have reported it numerous times. What does your team actually do!!! It is in a disgusting state and needs to be removed now!
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Witherston Way, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Verges around wooden fence lines, 4th June
item.description:
There are wooden posts in the park and the mud verges here are very dangerous huge chunks are coming away leading to it being dangerous for children exploring this area with large stones/rocks sticking out and steep drops that need to be sorted, the grassed areas at the top end of the play park running behind the slide, roundabout, bench to the main street gate
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Imperial Way, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bike blocking pavement, 4th June
item.description:
As described in title

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24-35, Hanson Close, Balham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown grass containing nettles and generally very overgrown, 3rd June
item.description:
Grassed areas inside the play park are overgrown containing nettles which can be hurtful for children who play there
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Imperial Way, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recycling overflow, 3rd June
item.description:
The recycling bin to Garden place gets full too quickly, as soonas it’s emptied it’s full again , the recycling bin provided is not enough for the amount of people living at Garden place Haggerston rd, we need another recycling bin, it only gets emptied 1
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Samuel Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped fridges on pavement, 3rd June
item.description:
These two items have been there for weeks and represent a health and safety hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Oakhurst Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo, 3rd June
item.description:
There has been dog poo left for a few days now
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Worsley Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Debris from burnt out van, 3rd June
item.description:
Please can all the debris and parts of the burnout van be cleaned up as this is the only access entrance to a disabled person home & they will damage their mobility scooter if the ride over all this mess. This is stopping them from being able to leave their house so please can you make this urgent!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 163, Star Lane, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees blocking street lights, 3rd June
item.description:
Trees all along Daniel Close n18 are blocking street lights at night as there are only 3 it's extremely dark . This has been reported in April but nothing has been done also the clearing the rubbish behind fence which I was told tenders had been put out to clear by Ergin Erbil

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Daniel Close, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This bin is always overflowing and there is flytipping, 3rd June
item.description:
As above, always flytipping and overflowing bin. Please sort this out it’s a health hazard
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): De Beauvoir Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping again outside debeauvoir primary school, 3rd June
item.description:
Again there has been flytipping here and the foxes then rip open the rubbish. And spread it across Tottenham Road. It might be the people in the school for security reasons.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): De Beauvoir Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside number 7, 3rd June
item.description:
Paint spill has been there for weeks really needs to be cleaned

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kenilworth Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish still all over the bus stop desite me reortong on Monday, 3rd June
item.description:
I sent a report Monday and received a notification saying it would be cleared ASAP. Children walk past to school. This is a health hazard as nappies are on the floor. It is disgusting.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Torridon Road, Catford South, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van with no front number plate parked on kerb and double yellow lines, 3rd June
item.description:
Van illegally parked with no front number plate also on kerb

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Vanbrugh Terrace, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet
